claim: PostHog offers a free tier that does not require a credit card.
The AI installation command for PostHog is "npx @posthog/wizard".
PostHog supports integration with Next.js, React, Python, and 21 additional technologies.
PostHog provides a managed data warehouse as part of its platform.
PostHog's product includes feature flags, A/B testing, and experiments without code.
PostHog is a SOC 2 and HIPAA compliant company.
PostHog's website indicates the company was established by 2026.
PostHog lists 24 customers who use its products.
Customers use a variety of PostHog products including Experiments, Product Analytics, Feature Flags, Session Replay, Surveys, AI, Error Tracking, CDP, Managed warehouse, and more.
The PostHog customers page provides filters to view how customers use different products.
PostHog includes case study links for several customers on its customers page.
PostHog’s customers page is part of the PostHog website and includes links to product documentation and pricing.
98% of PostHog customers use the product for free
PostHog offers a free tier of 1 million events per month for Product Analytics
PostHog charges $0.00005 per event for Product Analytics beyond the free tier
PostHog offers a free tier of 5,000 recordings per month for Session Replay
PostHog charges $0.005 per recording for Session Replay beyond the free tier
PostHog offers a free tier of 1 million requests per month for Feature Flags
PostHog charges $0.0001 per request for Feature Flags beyond the free tier
PostHog offers a free tier of 1 million rows per month for Managed Warehouse
PostHog charges $0.000015 per row for Managed Warehouse beyond the free tier
PostHog's product suite includes a data warehouse, 120+ sources/destinations, SQL editor, BI, data visualization, user activity feed, API, and webhooks
PostHog's support staff all have engineering backgrounds
PostHog provides public access to its company handbook, sales manual, and company strategy
PostHog's cloud is available in US (Virginia) and EU (Frankfurt) regions
PostHog is incorporated as PostHog, Inc. in 2026
PostHog offers a free "Get started" tier.

PostHog provides product analytics, session replay, feature flags, surveys, managed warehouse, realtime destinations, error tracking, CDP, web analytics, experiments, AI, observability, logs, workflows, inbox, and endpoints.
PostHog documentation includes an AI assistant that can answer questions without reading the docs.
PostHog can be accessed via Slack, web browser, and integrated into tools like Claude, Code, and Cursor.
PostHog is a company incorporated as PostHog, Inc. and the site is copyrighted in 2026.
More than 90% of companies use PostHog for free.
PostHog's free tier includes 1 project, 1-year data retention, unlimited team members, and community support.
PostHog Cloud offers cloud regions in US (Virginia) and EU (Frankfurt).
Product Analytics pricing is usage‑based at $0.00005 per event for the first 1 million events after the free tier.
Session Replay pricing is $0.005 per recording for 5‑15 k recordings after the free tier of 5 k recordings.
Feature Flags pricing is $0.0001 per request for the first 1 million requests after the free tier.
Surveys pricing starts at $0.10 per response after the free tier of 1.5 k responses.
Managed warehouse pricing starts at $0.000015 per row for 1‑10 million rows after the free tier of 1 million rows.
PostHog AI credits are provided free up to 500 credits (worth $5), then $0.01 per credit thereafter.
Logs pricing is $0.25 per GB for the first 50 GB after the free tier of 50 GB.
Workflows email sending is $0.003 per email for 10‑50 k emails after the free tier of 10 k emails.
Inbox pricing is $15 per PR after the free tier of 3 PRs.
All plans include unlimited team members and API access.
Standard support (email, Slack) is available for plans over $2 k per month, offering 6 projects and 7‑year data retention.
PostHog offers platform packages with dedicated support, RBAC, and SSO enforcement.
PostHog's main repository has 35,223 stars on GitHub.
The main PostHog repository has 2,902 forks.
The main PostHog repository is primarily written in Python.
The main PostHog repository has 3,931 open issues as of the latest push.
The latest commit to the main PostHog repository was pushed on 2026-06-29 at 11:11:52 UTC.

PostHog describes itself as an all-in-one developer platform offering product analytics, web analytics, session replay, error tracking, feature flags, experimentation, surveys, a data warehouse, a CDP, and an AI product assistant.
PostHog's documentation repository (posthog.com) has 1,057 stars and 837 forks.
The posthog.com repository is primarily written in TypeScript.
The HouseWatch repository has 620 stars and 43 forks and is written in TypeScript.
Primary programming languages across PostHog's GitHub organization are C, Python, and TypeScript.
PostHog was part of Y Combinator's Winter 2020 (W20) batch.
PostHog launched its MVP on Hacker News in 2020, four weeks after starting code development.
PostHog is used by more than 190,254 teams.
James Hawkins and Tim Glaser are co-CEOs of PostHog.
PostHog has never had layoffs.
All members of PostHog's support team have engineering backgrounds.
PostHog's codebase is open source and publicly auditable.
PostHog maintains a public product roadmap that users can vote on.
PostHog currently offers more than 10 paid products.
PostHog aims to reach a company valuation of at least $100 billion.
PostHog's pricing matches the cheapest major competitor and includes generous free tiers.
PostHog does not increase prices annually, unlike Salesforce which raises prices by 6% each year.
PostHog does not use auto‑renewal contracts or sleazy renewal tactics.

verdict.bull: Large developer community and open-source adoption could drive future enterprise sales.
verdict.bear: Heavy reliance on free users and unclear market positioning may hinder revenue growth.

risk_matrix.reason: Generous free tier and usage-based pricing may limit revenue per user, but clear pricing model and multiple products reduce risk.
Open source with active commits, but 3,931 open issues could signal maintenance challenges.
Market facts are thin; only case study links provided, no competitive landscape or market size data.
Large user base and GitHub stars, but 98% free users suggests low conversion to paid.

section_summaries.overview: PostHog is a YC-backed open-source developer platform offering a suite of analytics and feature tools with a generous free tier and usage-based pricing.
section_summaries.engineering: The codebase is open source, primarily Python/TypeScript, actively maintained with many open issues, and supports multiple integrations and cloud regions.
section_summaries.market: Market positioning is not detailed in provided facts, with only case study links hinting at some customer validation.
section_summaries.traction: PostHog boasts over 190k teams and 35k GitHub stars, but 98% of customers use the free tier, indicating low monetization.
